event: admitted to the unit with abdominal distress
medical history of two kidney transplantations
current allograft functioning with eGFR 60 ml/min/1.73 m²
computed tomographic scan revealed free intra-abdominal air
initial blood cultures positive with Alistipes onderdonkii
vitamin C 1.5 g IV four times daily for 4 days
vitamin B1 200 mg IV three times daily for 3 days
tubular proteinuria 750 mg alpha-1-microglobulin per g creatinine
urine albumin 180 mg per g creatinine
acute tubular damage in context of sepsis
kidney biopsy showed acute tubular injury
no double contours of glomerular basement membranes
diagnosed with oxalate nephropathy secondary to vitamin C
citrate-based continuous renal replacement therapy continued
second episode of acute kidney injury
kidney allograft function recovered to eGFR 45–50 ml/min/1.73 m²
